title: Observability
scenario: Diagnose a hidden fault from telemetry alone against a running clock.
prerequisites: Complete the preceding chapter and preserve its learner-owned pass evidence.
Wait until the Chapter 08 deterministic red instrument and cumulative start checkpoint are published.
outcomes: Follow one run across gateway, queue, model, and tool boundaries.
Localize the injected fault without reading test source.
Prove observability reveals control flow without leaking content.
objectives: Follow one run across gateway, queue, model, and tool boundaries.
Localize the injected fault without reading test source.
Prove observability reveals control flow without leaking content.

steps.title: Observe
steps.body:
### Observe

Reproduce and preserve evidence for this incident once its deterministic instrument is published: Diagnose a hidden fault from telemetry alone against a running clock.
steps.success_criteria: The published incident can be reproduced and its initial evidence can be preserved without changing the system.

actions.description: Plan the observe activity after this lab's cumulative checkpoint and deterministic incident are published.

hints.prompt: What invariant must the observe step protect, and what evidence would establish it?
Use docs/scale/08-observability.md to locate the relevant boundary and evidence; do not infer or apply a future repair.
Compare the available options against this step's success criteria and record the tradeoff without consulting a reference solution.

steps.title: Explain
steps.body:
### Explain

Explain the observed control-flow, state, authority, and failure boundaries without reading or changing a future solution.
steps.success_criteria: The learner names the failing boundary, affected invariant, and evidence that supports the explanation.

actions.description: Plan the explain activity after this lab's cumulative checkpoint and deterministic incident are published.

hints.prompt: What invariant must the explain step protect, and what evidence would establish it?
Use docs/scale/08-observability.md to locate the relevant boundary and evidence; do not infer or apply a future repair.
Compare the available options against this step's success criteria and record the tradeoff without consulting a reference solution.

steps.title: Decide
steps.body:
### Decide

Choose correlation fields and safe telemetry content before instrumenting.
steps.success_criteria: A learner-authored decision compares viable options and records the chosen tradeoff without copying a reference solution.

steps.body:
### Repair

This step remains unavailable until the chapter checkpoint and deterministic grader are published.

steps.title: Prove
steps.body:
### Prove

Run the published grader, replay the closest realistic user path, and curate only redacted evidence.
steps.success_criteria: The planned deterministic and realistic-path proof is named without publishing a grader or executable command.

actions.description: Plan the prove activity after this lab's cumulative checkpoint and deterministic incident are published.

hints.prompt: What invariant must the prove step protect, and what evidence would establish it?
Use docs/scale/08-observability.md to locate the relevant boundary and evidence; do not infer or apply a future repair.
Compare the available options against this step's success criteria and record the tradeoff without consulting a reference solution.

completion.planned_proof: After publication, prove Chapter 08 with its deterministic incident plus a realistic replay in an isolated worktree.
planned_decision_artifacts: A learner-authored Chapter 08 decision record that compares viable approaches and preserves earlier invariants.
